figures.caption: Figure 1: Performance comparison of Temporal Global Policy Optimization (TGPO), GRPO, and GSPO on five egocentric benchmarks.
Table 7: Performance comparison on EgoTempo benchmark.
Table 1: Performance comparison of our method TGPO with two popular RL-based optimization methods and chain-of-thought (CoT) reasoning across egocentric benchmarks.
Figure 4: Qualitative comparison between TGPO and GRPO on the EgoSchema Benchmark.
Figure 3: Test reward over the 3000 training steps. The reward curves are reported on four benchmarks across GRPO, GSPO, and our two TGPO variants.

Figure 2: An overview of our proposed TGPO. In the upper half, the original video is passed into the policy model, and a group of responses are sampled. Rewards are assigned to each response in the group. In the bottom half, the policy model takes in a shuffled video and performs greedy decoding to generate responses. The reward model assigns scores to responses without temporal information.
